124047.explanation: In exceptional cases, that might be an option. More important is that major decisions have as much support as possible, both in society and in the council.

124052.explanation: We find it important that employment matches our labor market. We seek companies that provide jobs. A distribution center contributes only limitedly to that. And currently, we still have a large distribution center vacant.

124058.explanation: Especially now with the fireworks ban.

124059.explanation: We believe the polluter should pay. If collection is free, the bill ultimately ends up with all citizens.

124062.explanation: That possibility already exists and we want to maintain it.

124064.explanation: Agreed, good education belongs in good buildings.

124065.explanation: There is a budget for culture, which can also be used for music lessons. It is up to the schools and parents to arrange that properly.

124069.explanation: In places where there is nuisance, that would be a good idea.

124074.explanation: For BGL, it is especially important that extra housing is built, but also that space for greenery remains in the centers.

124075.explanation: Where possible, that could certainly help. But it should be from the perspective of those involved and not from economic pressure.

124076.explanation: That only causes people more problems, and that is exactly what BGL does not want.

124077.explanation: Steenwijkerland is at 130% and is thus a very social municipality.

124079.explanation: With the rise of more electric cars, that is a good idea, but there must remain sufficient room for cars running on fossil fuels.

124080.explanation: That strongly depends on the situation. Sometimes it is necessary, but very often not.

124081.explanation: With the shortages in healthcare, that is not a feasible wish. Certainly not at the municipal level.

124085.explanation: BGL believes that tourists should contribute proportionally.

124088.explanation: We find it important that housing remains available for our own residents. At the same time, we have a responsibility for our new Dutch citizens. Therefore, to a limited extent and no more than 10% of the vacated homes.

124089.explanation: BGL believes that the user must pay. Otherwise the bill falls on all residents.

124091.explanation: It is important that children learn to swim. But it is complicated if schools are located far from the swimming pool.

124094.explanation: BGL aims for a diverse housing offer. 30% social housing fits well within that.

124095.explanation: BGL is in favor of allowing more high-rise buildings in the larger centers.

124096.explanation: It often also offers opportunities for young starters and/or students.

124098.explanation: It is time that the ring roads in Ossenzijl and the access road in Blokzijl are constructed. BGL has been fighting for this for years.

124099.explanation: BGL has so far been cautious about building on the Kamp, but we also see the pressure on the housing market increasing and the water and soil regulations make building elsewhere less promising. Building with attention to historical values.

124100.explanation: BGL supports focusing on housing for our own residents, but in some places in the municipality BGL still sees room for new holiday parks. Especially also to maintain facilities (also for our own people).

124101.explanation: BGL is very cautious when it comes to wind energy with very large turbines. Partly because the province and national government keep changing the policy. BGL does see room for generation by smaller wind turbines with 100% local ownership.

124047.explanation: The resident/voter has given a mandate to the municipal council. Residents can always approach the council and executive board in various ways. Good accessibility for us as a municipality and council is very important. Currently, it is sufficient and that must be maintained.

124051.explanation: The proceeds of course go primarily to the initiators, and the investments and costs belong to them as well. However, there is a role for the municipality to possibly stimulate this.

124052.explanation: Only with sufficient availability of business park land; currently, there is no space for this.

124056.explanation: Only when it does not cause major nuisance. Prohibiting this in advance for the entire municipality goes too far for us.

124058.explanation: Depending on the center, and the possibilities and needs.

124059.explanation: There must be sufficient facilities to be able to dispose of the waste.

124062.explanation: There are already financial schemes for this, it is important that this is made known to residents and associations.

124064.explanation: Much has already been done, and there is an educational housing program that includes all schools. This is well organized in our municipality.

124065.explanation: There are financial arrangements that can help cover the costs.

124068.explanation: Only when it can be demonstrated that the tasks cannot be performed. This falls under the municipality's operations.

124069.explanation: In some places, this can be a good measure. So we do not want to rule it out.

124074.explanation: There is high demand for housing, while the rural area is developing. Of course, it must be appropriate and not restrict agricultural businesses in their development opportunities.

124075.explanation: Being active where possible is always good. It benefits society and the individual, and can certainly offer opportunities, including for reintegration into the workforce.

124076.explanation: There is already policy for minimum income support. Anyone can end up in a hopeless financial situation. It is also part of our care for one another.

124077.explanation: There is already policy here as well. But once again, if residents are in trouble, they must be well informed about the available options.

124079.explanation: This goes without saying. Although this transition is not happening very quickly yet, it is progressing.

124080.explanation: This depends on the situation. Where necessary, it can be a good tool, but not as a default.

124081.explanation: Availability and quality take precedence here. If the option is available and a choice can be made, then that is fine.

124085.explanation: Tourists want and can contribute to the environment in which they recreate.

124088.explanation: If we can steer this, we should, also because our residents sometimes wait years on housing lists. Consultation with housing associations is important here.

124089.explanation: What we do find important is that opening hours are generous and the burdens low.

124091.explanation: We are neutral on this. Our municipality has a lot of water, and parents are also willing to invest in this.

124094.explanation: Agreements have already been made about this in the housing agenda. However, there can be situations where demand for social rental housing is lower, in which case deviations should be possible.

124095.explanation: Depending on the town and location. However, this can be a good addition to our housing agenda.

124096.explanation: This can definitely offer a solution in certain situations. Safety measures must be in order, though.

124098.explanation: Maximum lobbying is necessary, and both the national and provincial governments can contribute effectively.

124099.explanation: Not by default. However, we cannot exclude that this may become a realistic option in the future. But first, all other options must be addressed.

124100.explanation: Ruling it out by default is not wise; however, it depends on location and region.

124101.explanation: We do not need to actively pursue this, unless we are specifically asked to. Location remains leading, and revenues should stay within the area.

124047.explanation: We choose real participation: early involvement, neighborhood meetings, and participation that has influence. A referendum sounds democratic but oversimplifies complex choices and often intensifies divisions rather than cooperation.

124051.explanation: Local energy initiatives strengthen support and ensure revenues remain in the community. The municipality must actively support residents who invest together in sustainable energy and help make plans feasible.

124052.explanation: Large-scale distribution centers put great pressure on the landscape, traffic, and livability, while adding little to the local community. We choose careful use of space and protection of the rural area.

124056.explanation: Wood burning causes serious air pollution and health complaints, especially for children and the elderly. We choose clean air and a healthy living environment. This requires reducing polluting forms of heating.

124058.explanation: A municipal fireworks show is safer, cleaner, and causes less nuisance than widespread private fireworks. This way, we celebrate New Year's together with less injury, less damage, and more attention to people, animals, and the environment.

124059.explanation: Waste policy must be sustainable and fair. Collecting bulky waste for free for everyone encourages wastefulness. However, the municipality must ensure that people who cannot bring it themselves receive good and affordable support.

124062.explanation: Sports and exercise are essential for health, well-being, and participation. Lack of money should not be a reason to sit on the sidelines. The municipality must ensure that people with low incomes can also participate in sports and be part of a club.

124064.explanation: Good school buildings are crucial for equality of opportunity and health. Through the Integral Housing Plan, we invest systematically in safe, sustainable, and future-proof schools. This gives children in Steenwijkerland the best start.

124065.explanation: Music education increases creativity, self-confidence, and equal opportunities. Talent should not depend on parents' income. With municipal support, we ensure every child has the chance to develop culturally.

124068.explanation: More enforcers do not solve the causes of insecurity. We choose prevention: invest in youth work, neighborhood work, and early detection. This prevents problems instead of enforcing after the fact.

124069.explanation: Camera surveillance addresses symptoms, not causes. We choose prevention: strong neighborhoods, youth work, and social cohesion. This sustainably increases safety without residents constantly feeling watched.

124074.explanation: To protect nature and landscape, we mainly build within existing villages. Infill and redevelopment keep towns livable. But if that is insufficient, building outside village boundaries is sometimes necessary as a last resort, carefully and limited.

124075.explanation: A social assistance benefit is a safety net, not a punishment. People are helped forward with trust, guidance, and perspective, not coercion. Voluntary work is encouraged; mandatory work increases stress and distance from genuine participation.

124076.explanation: Exemption prevents people with low income from falling deeper into trouble. Stopping it increases poverty and debt. We choose security of existence and a municipality that supports people instead of undermining them.

124077.explanation: Too many people now just fall outside the schemes, while they do struggle financially. By raising the income limit, we ensure help reaches everyone who needs it and prevent debt and stress.

124079.explanation: Electric driving helps reduce air pollution and CO₂ emissions. More charging points make the transition feasible for everyone, including those without private driveways. This way, we accelerate sustainable mobility in a fair way.

124080.explanation: 30 km/h zones make streets safer and more livable. They reduce traffic casualties, noise nuisance, and provide more space for cyclists and pedestrians. This makes villages and neighborhoods more pleasant and safer for everyone.

124081.explanation: Care must unite, not divide. Organizing care based on religion or culture creates separate systems and increases inequality. We choose inclusive, person-centered care where everyone feels seen, without differences leading to exclusion.

124085.explanation: Tourism requires investments in facilities, accessibility, and livability. Tourist tax helps distribute these costs fairly. Reducing it means residents pay more, while visitors should contribute to the pressure tourism causes.

124088.explanation: People with a residence permit should be able to start integrating. Quick housing prevents pressure on shelters, promotes participation, and provides stability. This is humane and practical, while we also continue building more affordable homes for everyone.

124089.explanation: Free disposal at the recycling center prevents illegal dumping and encourages waste separation and reuse. Good environmental behavior should be rewarded, not fined. Together, this ensures a clean and sustainable living environment.

124091.explanation: Swimming skills are a basic skill and vital, especially in a water-rich municipality like Steenwijkerland. School swimming increases safety and equal opportunities. Every child must learn to swim, regardless of their parents' financial situation.

124094.explanation: The housing shortage mainly affects people with low or middle incomes. With at least 30% social rental housing, we ensure affordability, mixed neighborhoods, and real opportunities for starters and families. This way, we build fairly and sustainably.

124095.explanation: High-rise buildings help address the housing shortage and save space. By building higher in suitable locations, we protect the landscape and create more affordable homes. This requires careful integration but clear choices.

124096.explanation: Room-by-room rental often leads to exploitation, nuisance, and displacement in the housing market. By limiting this, we protect tenants, maintain living quality, and ensure homes remain available for families and starters.

124098.explanation: The bypass near Blokzijl is necessary for safety and livability and must be realized. For Ossenzijl, this is only possible with support from the province and national government. Large investments require customized solutions and cooperation-not solely municipal effort.

124099.explanation: Housing development on the Steenwijkerkamp is possible but not our first choice. Infill and building within the core take priority. If construction occurs here, it must be careful, limited, and in harmony with the landscape and cultural-historical qualities of the area.

124100.explanation: New holiday parks increase pressure on nature, landscape, and livability, while the housing shortage is severe. We choose quality over quantity: first improve existing parks and use space for housing, nature, and amenities for residents.

124101.explanation: Wind turbines can raise concerns about landscape and living environment. We take these seriously. At the same time, sustainable energy is essential. By placing wind turbines carefully and involving local residents, we take responsibility for a clean and future-proof energy supply.

124047.explanation: We believe it’s important that residents can express their opinions on important issues in various ways. There are different methods for this. The downside of a referendum is that turnout is usually low, which puts the representativeness of the outcome under pressure.

124051.explanation: The ambition is to generate more sustainable energy in the future. If residents initiate projects themselves, the municipality should support such initiatives. For the VVD, however, this support is not financial. We see more opportunities in fast permit issuance and administrative technical support.

124052.explanation: For the VVD, it is important that there remains enough space for entrepreneurs to grow. Therefore, we want to expand current and new business parks. If there is market demand for distribution centers, we will not block this as long as there is sufficient space for companies.

124056.explanation: As long as the burner uses a safe installation with dry wood that meets the stipulated requirements, wood burning falls within personal freedoms according to the VVD. Those causing nuisance without proper installations should be addressed.

124058.explanation: Fireworks and carbide shooting are part of Steenwijkerland’s identity. Within the new regulations, we hope there is as much room as possible for communities to organize collectively. We prefer to support initiatives emerging from society itself rather than from the municipality.

124059.explanation: Ultimately, nothing is free. Our principle is that the user pays. When a resident disposes of bulky waste, a cost-covering fee is charged so the user actually pays. Collection at home is an interesting idea, but it also involves additional costs.

124062.explanation: Anyone moving from benefits into work must always improve financially, and we want to tackle regulations that cause poverty traps. Such schemes sound sympathetic but at the same time create poverty traps for workers.

124064.explanation: Schools, in the VVD's view, are part of basic infrastructure and must be in order and safe. We encourage schools to collaborate under one roof in multifunctional buildings. This keeps the buildings affordable and allows sharing some costs.

124065.explanation: Music lessons are incredibly important for children’s development. However, according to the VVD, this is primarily a financial responsibility of the parents or guardians.

124068.explanation: We take a tough approach against nuisance caused by youth, drug-related crime, and vandalism. Neighborhood police officers and enforcement officers must be visibly present in neighborhoods and villages. Where necessary, we deploy camera surveillance in Steenwijk and industrial areas.

124069.explanation: We take a tough approach against nuisance caused by youth, drug-related crime, and vandalism. Neighborhood police officers and enforcement officers must be visibly present in neighborhoods and villages. Where necessary, we deploy camera surveillance in Steenwijk and industrial areas.

124074.explanation: Owning a home is the foundation of freedom and independence, according to the VVD. Young people must be able to start in their own municipality, families should be able to move to a suitable home, and seniors should find housing that fits their life stage. More space is needed for this.

124075.explanation: We expect everyone who can work or contribute to do so. For those who cannot (yet), we provide support to gradually participate. Those who do not work must provide a return service for their benefits.

124076.explanation: Work must pay off. Anyone moving from benefits into work must always improve financially, and we want to address schemes that cause poverty traps. These arrangements cause poverty traps. There should be a safety net for workers in poverty.

124077.explanation: Anyone moving from benefits into work must always improve financially, and we want to tackle regulations that cause poverty traps. These schemes cause poverty traps.

124079.explanation: Additionally, we want sufficient regular parking spaces. To maintain the same parking capacity, more parking spaces are needed.

124080.explanation: Safety, not speed, is our priority. If 50 km/h is safe, that is our preference. If lowering speed is better for safety, we are open to that.

124081.explanation: A large part of the municipal budget goes to the social domain; this is expected to increase in the coming years.

124085.explanation: We currently have a low tourist tax compared to neighboring municipalities. We do not consider further reduction responsible at this time.

124088.explanation: We want to halt priority housing allocation for newcomers. Status holders do not deserve priority for social rental housing over other housing seekers.

124089.explanation: Ultimately, nothing is free. Our principle is that the user pays. When a resident disposes of waste, a cost-covering fee is charged so the user actually pays.

124091.explanation: At its core, this is a financial responsibility of the parents or guardians.

124094.explanation: We encourage a mix of owner-occupied and rental housing. Progress is most important for us. If project feasibility calls for fewer social rentals and more owner-occupied homes, we opt for that to enable faster housing production.

124095.explanation: If spatially feasible, we are in favor of high-rise buildings.

124096.explanation: In some cases, individual rooms can actually be a good, cheap temporary solution.

124098.explanation: Good infrastructure and safe roads are decisive for livable cities and communities. Therefore, we want to continue constructing access roads like those near Blokzijl and Ossenzijl. This keeps centers safe, improves traffic flow, and provides an economic boost.

124099.explanation: To relieve pressure on the housing market, we fully focus on large-scale new building locations. De Kamp is very suitable for housing development. We want to start at De Schans, while keeping an eye on traffic pressure, nature values, and the relationship with Southveen residents.

124100.explanation: Tourists enable certain amenities in communities, like supermarkets and restaurants, to remain open. We are open to new, high-quality holiday parks.

124101.explanation: We believe wind energy development in the region should mainly take place around Zwolle. With Natura 2000 areas, a National Park, and unique landscapes, there is no place for wind energy in Steenwijkerland.

124047.explanation: The municipal council, as a reflection of society, is elected to make these decisions. Residents have sufficient opportunities to express their wishes on specific decisions.

124051.explanation: In this way, contribution to sustainability can be made. However, it must fit within existing policies and must not cause nuisance, such as solar panels on the ground or wind turbines.

124052.explanation: There may be opportunities for this in the industrial area around Steenwijk. However, space is limited, so not everything is simply possible. But banning is the extreme opposite.

124056.explanation: It is a great option to heat with wood from reed beds and other nature maintenance. So it makes sense that this remains possible here.

124058.explanation: Currently, there is a nationwide fireworks ban. The possibilities that will remain later are not yet clear. For many residents, a fireworks show is not really an alternative. Especially due to the many villages in Steenwijkerland, it is unfeasible to do this in multiple locations.

124059.explanation: It is fine for it to cost something to collect bulky waste. However, it must stay within limits; otherwise, you create a litter problem. Bringing waste to multiple locations in the municipality has long been a wish of the CDA.

124062.explanation: There are other means available to help this group of people. It is important to encourage these individuals to start exercising.

124064.explanation: This was already planned, but it turns out that the funds previously allocated are insufficient. An improved plan must be developed to continue investing in good school buildings.

124065.explanation: It is questionable in what form this should be done. But by keeping music lesson costs low, you encourage children to make music, thereby stimulating their creativity and development.

124068.explanation: Safety deserves more attention. This can be done by deploying public order officers, but also with extra neighborhood police officers and simplifying legal procedures.

124069.explanation: It has not been proven that camera surveillance is currently inadequate. The CDA is cautious about recording everything on camera. But privacy is also important in this. If specific locations are found lacking, then more camera surveillance is definitely an option.

124074.explanation: There are good opportunities to build outside the existing village boundaries as well. For the CDA, priority must be given to building within the core.

124075.explanation: This is not always possible and must be taken into account. But basically, it works well that these people contribute to society.

124076.explanation: Stopping this completely is not wise. There is a reason why it happens. However, it remains a matter of customization. Automatically waiving taxes is also not desirable.

124077.explanation: The current threshold is basically well chosen. If poverty were to increase, this would be a reason to take action.

124079.explanation: The use of electric cars is increasing. So having more places to charge would be logical.

124080.explanation: Within the municipality, there is considerable diversity among villages. There are enough places where a 50 km/h limit works well. Therefore, it is not necessary to implement this everywhere by default.

124081.explanation: Basically, the CDA finds it important that sufficient space is offered for assistance based on religious beliefs. However, in practice, this is difficult to implement and it is not really the municipality's task to facilitate all of this.

124085.explanation: The current level is fine. It does not need to be increased, except for inflation correction.

124088.explanation: We must mainly look at what our municipal obligation is from the national government. Then it can be assessed where these people should live.

124089.explanation: People are used to the fact that this costs money, but the rates are not very high, so adjustment is not necessary. The CDA would like to invest these revenues in small locations spread throughout the municipality where bulky waste or garden waste can be brought.

124091.explanation: In a water-rich municipality like Steenwijkerland, it is important that residents can swim. Furthermore, swimming through school also plays an important role in children's personal development.

124094.explanation: This is according to the agreements and guidelines under which new construction is currently taking place.

124095.explanation: By making more use of height, more space for homes is created.

124096.explanation: There is no really clear insight into this.

124098.explanation: The decision to build the Blokzijl bypass has basically already been made. Not so for Ossenzijl. If this is realized, the municipality will need to reserve sufficient budget for it.

124099.explanation: But only when other locations within the city of Steenwijk itself have been optimally used. And when it is well integrated into the landscape.

124100.explanation: The CDA believes that this door should not be simply closed. However, space for this is very limited.

124101.explanation: Steenwijkerland is not really a municipality with suitable locations for wind turbines. We want to focus more on innovation, such as hydrogen. Solar panels on roofs remain a good alternative. Moreover, network issues must be resolved first.

124047.explanation: Citizen panels, consultation evenings, and consultations should be maximally used as forms of participation because they allow more room for nuance and dialogue. These should take place before decision-making processes. That is where residents can express their opinions on important topics.

124051.explanation: Residents who start an energy cooperative to jointly generate or store energy deserve financial support during the startup phase. After that, the initiative must be independently sustainable.

124052.explanation: The Christian Union wants to stimulate the business climate for companies that also contribute to employment. Distribution centers take up a lot of space but comparatively contribute little to local employment.

124056.explanation: We are a rural municipality with many homes, especially in the countryside, that are partly heated with wood-burning stoves and fireplaces. We want to continue offering this option. However, nuisance and health risks for neighbors must be limited.

124058.explanation: The Christian Union believes that professional fireworks shows can be an alternative now that there is a fireworks ban, but the costs can be shared or borne by organizers, sponsors, or partnerships.

124059.explanation: The Christian Union endorses the principle 'the polluter pays.' There is no such thing as free waste disposal. Offering bulky waste disposal for free means all households would have to pay together via waste collection fees, which would then increase.

124062.explanation: It is important that everyone can participate, including having the opportunity to practice a sport. Sport contributes to health and social bonding with others. Within current poverty and minimum income policy frameworks, memberships of sports clubs can also be paid for.

124064.explanation: For the Christian Union, good school buildings are very important for a healthy learning and working environment, with attention to sustainability as well. Therefore, the municipality must continue implementing the integrated housing plan for schools.

124065.explanation: It is important that everyone can participate. Many parents can afford music lessons themselves. Therefore, support is only needed for residents who cannot pay due to their financial situation. This is part of our poverty and minimum income policy.

124068.explanation: Additional public order officers for supervision and enforcement are important. With the current number of officers, not all tasks can be properly performed. The extra deployment is needed not only for 'safety' but also for livability and approachability.

124069.explanation: The Christian Union sees camera surveillance as a means to increase safety but emphasizes that this must always be balanced with privacy, clear rules, temporary use, and good control over the use of the images.

124074.explanation: The focus must first be on building within the city. Given the housing demand and additional requirements for climate-adaptive and nature-inclusive building, it will be impossible to realize everything within the city. Expansion outside village boundaries must be done carefully and integrated into the landscape.

124075.explanation: The Christian Union finds it important that people can develop themselves and participate in society, even if they receive social assistance. We do not want to make this mandatory, but we want to encourage it.

124076.explanation: The Christian Union is proud of the municipality's generous poverty and minimum income policy, which also includes waivers. Waivers prevent the worsening of debt problems.

124077.explanation: The income threshold in the municipality of Steenwijkerland is higher than in most municipalities. We want to maintain this. We consider it even more important that the regulations are simple to understand and use, and that residents are informed about them.

124079.explanation: In the context of the transition to more electric transportation, more parking spaces with charging points must be created, distributed throughout the entire municipality.

124080.explanation: For safety reasons and considering that cars are guests, it is good to adjust the speed limits in many locations. This does not apply to places where, for example, bicycle paths are separated from the roadway. Measures should be tailored to the specific location to determine what is most appropriate.

124081.explanation: It is important that all residents have access to identity-driven care organizations. There must remain sufficient freedom of choice in this.

124085.explanation: The tourist tax is at an acceptable level and can increase with inflation. This provides predictability for entrepreneurs. The Christian Union believes that tourists should also contribute to the facilities in our municipality.

124088.explanation: To relieve the asylum centers, it is important to provide housing for status holders linked to the municipality. The Christian Union supports the current approach, where 10% of social rental houses that become available are reserved for this target group.

124089.explanation: The Christian Union stands behind the principle 'the polluter pays.' There is no such thing as free waste collection. Offering it for free will affect the height of the waste disposal charges, causing all residents to contribute.

124091.explanation: Water safety is important in a water-rich municipality like Steenwijkerland. Although school swimming is not a municipal task, we ensure that no child is left out. Parents who cannot afford swimming lessons are supported through poverty and minimum income policies.

124094.explanation: The Christian Union is in favor of a mix of housing in new residential areas. At least 30% social rental and social ownership homes are part of this.

124095.explanation: The Christian Union wants to allow high-rise buildings that are appropriate for the environment, such as on the outskirts and in the station area. Within the city, medium-rise buildings (up to 5 floors) can be considered.

124096.explanation: In addition to houses, there are groups who are fine with just a room. The Christian Union supports the current policy whereby renting a dwelling to more than one household is only allowed with an approved permit.

124098.explanation: The Christian Union believes the municipality should co-finance the bypass roads of Blokzijl and Ossenzijl due to livability and traffic safety. These are large sums that the municipality cannot pay entirely by itself. A bypass for Ossenzijl is only possible with co-financing.

124099.explanation: In the coming years, we focus on building around Steenwijk in Steenwijk-Zuidoost, Tuk, and inner-city areas including the station area. Building on the Kamp is possible in the long term under conditions including proper landscape integration.

124100.explanation: The Christian Union emphasizes the importance of care for nature and landscape, livability for local residents, and careful use of space. It believes that recreation expansion is only responsible if it is demonstrably sustainable and does not put additional pressure on nature, infrastructure, or the housing market.

124101.explanation: The space for larger wind turbines is limited in Steenwijkerland. We support installing wind turbines near industrial areas. We are against wind turbines near vulnerable nature areas and want to exclude meadow bird areas.

124047.explanation: Residents must feel heard and be able to participate in major decisions. D66 mainly focuses on participation and dialogue; a referendum can be an additional tool. However, it must be used carefully and explained well.

124051.explanation: D66 encourages local, sustainable grassroots initiatives. The municipality can primarily help with knowledge, guidance, and removing obstacles. This often works better than just giving money.

124052.explanation: We do not want unnecessary and empty block-shaped buildings in our landscape. Space in Steenwijkerland is scarce and must be used carefully. New business activities must add real value.

124056.explanation: We recognize the nuisance and health risks, but also that for some people this is part of their home. A total ban goes too far, but doing nothing also isn't an option. Education, discouragement, and tailored solutions are better suited here.

124058.explanation: Safety and livability are paramount around New Year's Eve. The municipality should primarily facilitate so that a fireworks ban can be effectively enforced. By facilitating, fireworks shows can be possible in multiple centers.

124059.explanation: Free collection is expensive and not sustainable. We believe the polluter should pay and that reuse must be stimulated. The current method is fairer and better for the environment.

124062.explanation: Participation in sports is important for health and social contacts. This is well arranged via existing minimum income schemes. D66 finds it important to maintain this policy.

124064.explanation: Investing in education is always good. At the same time, the municipality must be realistic: not everything can happen at once. Therefore, this must be phased through the education housing plan.

124065.explanation: Music lessons contribute to children's development. We think support is mainly needed for families who cannot afford it themselves. This can be arranged through existing poverty policy schemes.

124068.explanation: Visible enforcement contributes to safety and trust in the neighborhood. We believe the number of community enforcement officers should match local safety challenges. Prevention and enforcement go hand in hand.

124069.explanation: Safety is important, but camera surveillance is not a standard solution. We prefer visible enforcement and prevention. Cameras should only be used if there is truly no other option.

124074.explanation: We want to use space sparingly, but the possibility of building outside village boundaries must exist. Sometimes this is necessary for the housing task. This always requires careful consideration.

124075.explanation: We find it important that people participate in society according to their ability. This must always be appropriate to a person's situation and possibilities. It should never become coercion or punishment.

124076.explanation: We believe that people with low incomes should not get into additional difficulties. Remission ensures that everyone can continue to participate. We must maintain this policy.

124077.explanation: We find it important to help people who really need it. The current threshold aligns sufficiently with this. First, ensure existing arrangements are well utilized.

124079.explanation: The switch to electric driving requires sufficient charging points. We believe expansion should happen where there is demonstrable need. This way, we handle public space carefully.

124080.explanation: Lower speeds lead to more traffic safety and livability. We support 30 km/h zones in residential areas. This must be logical and well designed.

124081.explanation: Good care must be accessible to everyone. However, the choice of care provider is not up to the municipality. Therefore, we do not see this as a municipal responsibility.

124085.explanation: Tourists use facilities and should contribute fairly. We find it logical that the tourist tax goes up rather than down. That helps maintain livability.

124088.explanation: Status holders have the right to a quick and good start in our society. Priority helps relieve shelters and gives people perspective. At the same time, it remains important to build enough homes for everyone.

124089.explanation: Free disposal often leads to more waste and higher costs. We believe waste separation and reuse should be encouraged. A fair contribution fits with this.

124091.explanation: Swimming skills are a basic skill, especially in a water-rich municipality. We see school swimming as a good investment in safety and equal opportunities. This deserves municipal support.

124094.explanation: Affordable housing is an important principle for D66. At least 30% social rental ensures a balanced housing stock. This aligns with current policy.

124095.explanation: Utilizing upward space can help with the housing task. We believe this must fit the environment. Building heights up to about five stories are a good guideline.

124096.explanation: We do not see a total ban as necessary. Good rooming-in policy is important to protect housing quality. There must remain room for good and affordable living.

124098.explanation: These are important projects for safety and accessibility. We believe the municipality should actively commit to these. Where necessary, co-financing must be sought.

124099.explanation: For the housing task, this is a suitable location. At the same time, we must be sparing with space and avoid unnecessary overbuilding. Limited and careful development fits with attention to the landscape and history.

124100.explanation: We must be careful with our landscape and living environment. More holiday parks create extra pressure on nature and towns. D66 chooses quality over further growth.

124101.explanation: The energy transition also requires local choices. We believe Steenwijkerland should contribute with a few wind turbines. This must be done carefully, considering local residents and encouraging local participation.

124047.explanation: Veur Elkaar believes residents should have more direct influence over decisions that have major and lasting impact on their living environment. A referendum can be an additional democratic instrument, alongside representative democracy.

124051.explanation: Veur Elkaar supports financial assistance for joint local energy initiatives so that the energy transition is by and for the residents.

124052.explanation: For Veur Elkaar, careful use of space is central. Scarce space must be used for functions that truly contribute to housing, livability, and a future-proof local economy. Distribution centers usually do not fit that.

124056.explanation: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves must remain allowed, with focus on responsible use and customization instead of bans.

124058.explanation: Veur Elkaar is against a municipal fireworks show and chooses safe alternatives such as a drone or light show.

124059.explanation: Veur Elkaar believes that waste collection should be affordable and fair but opposes completely free bulky waste collection at home. Free collection can lead to more waste, higher costs, and less conscious reuse.

124062.explanation: In Steenwijkerland, this is already arranged via the minimum income policy, allowing people with social assistance benefits to get support for sports and other social activities. This is a good and targeted way to prevent exclusion without stacking new schemes.

124064.explanation: Veur Elkaar believes good and safe school buildings are a basic requirement for quality education. A healthy indoor climate, safe buildings, and future-proof facilities contribute to equal opportunities, wellbeing, and children's learning performance.

124065.explanation: Veur Elkaar believes culture and creativity should be accessible to every child, regardless of their parents' income. Music lessons contribute to children's development: they stimulate concentration, self-confidence, social skills, and enjoyment of learning.

124068.explanation: Veur Elkaar believes visible and approachable enforcement contributes to safety, livability, and trust in public spaces. Additional public enforcement officers can help prevent nuisance, enforce rules, and quickly identify problems.

124069.explanation: Veur Elkaar values safety but is cautious about structurally expanding camera surveillance. Camera monitoring can be useful in specific situations and concrete problem areas, for example, with demonstrable and persistent nuisance or crime.

124074.explanation: In case of housing shortage, small-scale expansion outside village boundaries should be possible: adding a small street fitting the core.

124075.explanation: Veur Elkaar believes participation according to ability is important but opposes a mandatory obligation for everyone. Not every social assistance recipient is able to do community work due to health issues, caregiving responsibilities, or other circumstances.

124076.explanation: Veur Elkaar believes exemption from municipal taxes is a necessary safety net for residents with low incomes. It prevents people from getting into deeper trouble and contributes to livelihood security and social justice.

124077.explanation: Veur Elkaar sees more and more people just above current income limits who nevertheless struggle due to high housing costs and rising expenses. By raising the income limit, the municipality can prevent people from falling through the cracks.

124079.explanation: Veur Elkaar believes the transition to electric driving must be practical and feasible for residents. Enough charging points in neighborhoods, villages, and near facilities are essential.

124080.explanation: 30 km/h within built-up areas increases safety and livability in residential neighborhoods.

124081.explanation: Veur Elkaar believes care must be people-centered and appropriate. Trust, understanding, and mutual respect are essential for good care. The option to choose a care provider that aligns with someone’s faith, culture, or beliefs.

124085.explanation: Veur Elkaar believes tourism may contribute to the costs it causes. In a municipality with high recreational pressure, it is logical that visitors share in the maintenance of public spaces, infrastructure, enforcement, and livability.

124088.explanation: Veur Elkaar believes everyone has the right to a safe place to live but also acknowledges the severe housing shortage in Steenwijkerland. Young people and starters from our municipality often wait years for social rental housing.

124089.explanation: Veur Elkaar finds it important that waste can be easily and neatly delivered to prevent littering and illegal dumping. At the same time, Veur Elkaar believes waste collection must remain fair and affordable for all residents.

124091.explanation: Veur Elkaar considers school swimming essential for safety and equal opportunities. Not all parents can afford swimming lessons, while swimming skills are life-saving in a water-rich municipality like Steenwijkerland.

124094.explanation: Veur Elkaar preferably wants 40% social rental housing.

124095.explanation: Given the housing shortage, this is necessary. The sky is the limit!

124096.explanation: Veur Elkaar opposes a general ban on individual room rentals. Especially in times of housing shortage, room rentals can be a necessary and appropriate form of housing for starters, singles, and urgent seekers.

124098.explanation: These are needed to keep the area livable and safe. With a contribution from the national government.

124099.explanation: We will not build on this cultural-historical piece of land that also connects city and countryside.

124100.explanation: Veur Elkaar opposes large new holiday parks that strain livability, landscape, and facilities. At the same time, Veur Elkaar wants to allow space for small-scale and appropriate recreational initiatives that fit the environment.

124101.explanation: Wind turbines fit better at sea.

parties.name: PVBNI
parties.fullName: Partij voor Behoud van de Nederlandse Identiteit

124047.explanation: Residents should have direct influence on important decisions that affect their living environment. A local referendum strengthens democracy, increases trust in politics, and prevents decisions being made over residents' heads.

124051.explanation: The municipality should not provide subsidies for energy projects. Initiatives that are profitable should support themselves. Public funds belong to core tasks like safety and facilities, not risky or ideological projects.

124052.explanation: Large distribution centers consume landscape, cause extra traffic pressure, and often provide few permanent jobs for the region. The municipality must choose livability, space for residents and local entrepreneurs, not large-scale industrialization of the countryside.

124056.explanation: Residents should decide how to heat their homes. Open fireplaces and wood stoves are part of tradition and freedom of living. The municipality should not impose patronizing bans that limit residents’ daily lives.

124058.explanation: New Year’s Eve is a tradition of the residents, not of the government. The municipality should not spend public money on fireworks shows. Freedom and responsibility belong to the residents, not to government-organized replacements of traditions.

124059.explanation: Residents already pay sufficient waste disposal fees. Free bulky waste collection prevents illegal dumping and keeps the municipality clean. It is a basic provision that belongs to proper municipal management, without extra costs for residents.

124062.explanation: Sports and exercise are important, but the municipality should not make structural payments. Temporary support or customization for children and vulnerable people is possible, but always focused on participation and responsibility. No open-ended schemes, just common sense.

124064.explanation: Good school buildings are important, but not every building has the same priority. The municipality must invest where it is truly needed, focusing on safety and quality. No expensive prestige projects, but targeted and responsible use of taxpayer money.

124065.explanation: Music education is valuable, but not everything needs to be funded through municipal subsidies. The municipality can support schools and associations where necessary, with attention to talent and equal opportunities. Targeted support, not a general subsidy scheme.

124068.explanation: Safety is a core task of the municipality. More visible enforcement ensures order, prevents nuisance, and increases residents' sense of safety. Community officers support the police and make clear that rules are actually enforced.

124069.explanation: Camera surveillance helps reduce crime and nuisance and increases the chance of catching offenders. It must be used specifically at problem locations and always with clear rules. The safety of residents comes first, with respect for privacy.

124074.explanation: The landscape must be protected, but the housing shortage calls for realism. Infill development takes priority, but if that is insufficient, expansion outside village boundaries should be possible at carefully selected locations. Preserve character and provide space for housing, using common sense.

124075.explanation: Those who can work must also give back to society. Socially meaningful work enhances self-worth, aids reintegration, and is fair to working residents. Naturally, tailored solutions are needed for those who truly cannot work.

124076.explanation: Debt forgiveness should remain targeted at residents who genuinely cannot pay. At the same time, abuse must be prevented and working must always pay off. No automatic arrangements, but tailored solutions and clear limits.

124077.explanation: Help must reach residents who are truly in difficulty. Raising income limits arbitrarily makes programs costly and less targeted. The municipality must focus on tailored support, temporary assistance, and preventing discouragement of work.

124079.explanation: Charging points are needed for those who drive electric vehicles, but must not come at the expense of accessibility for other residents. The municipality should expand based on demand and prevent the unnecessary sacrifice of scarce parking spots. Freedom of choice and practical policy come first.

124080.explanation: The built-up area is not a uniform whole. In residential streets, 30 km/h can be logical, but on main roads within the built-up area, traffic flow must be maintained. A general reduction to 30 km/h everywhere is ineffective and causes unnecessary frustration.

124081.explanation: Good care begins with trust. If someone feels more comfortable with a care provider who matches their background, that choice should be possible. This strengthens the quality of care and respects personal freedom, as long as it remains within the law and available care.

124085.explanation: Tourism creates jobs and income for local entrepreneurs. Excessively high tourist taxes deter visitors and harm the local economy. The municipality should stimulate tourism and remain competitive, not hinder it with extra burdens.

124088.explanation: Social rent housing is scarce. Residents who live here, work here, and have been waiting for years should have priority. Status holders have a right to shelter, but not to priority over local residents. Fair policy prevents imbalance and frustration.

124089.explanation: Residents already pay waste disposal fees. Free access to the recycling center prevents illegal dumping and keeps the municipality clean. This is cheaper in the long run and fair to residents who properly dispose of their waste.

124091.explanation: Swimming skills are basic safety, especially in a water-rich municipality like Steenwijkerland. School swimming prevents accidents and ensures every child learns to swim, regardless of home situation. This is a wise investment in safety and responsibility.

124094.explanation: Social rental housing is necessary, but a fixed percentage of 30 percent is too rigid. The housing mix must match the local need per settlement. This way we keep space for starters, families, and elderly without unnecessarily delaying construction.

124095.explanation: High-rise apartment blocks do not suit the character of Steenwijkerland everywhere. Housing is needed but must fit the scale of villages and neighborhoods. In some places, height is acceptable, but large-scale urbanization harms livability and village identity.

124096.explanation: Room rentals can help with housing shortages but must not cause nuisance, subdivision, or damage to residential neighborhoods. The municipality must set clear rules and enforce them where necessary, without a general ban that affects good initiatives.

124098.explanation: Accessibility and traffic safety are important, but multi-million investments require careful consideration. First, it must be clear whether the problem will really be solved and if cheaper alternatives exist. Responsible investing, not a blank check.

124099.explanation: Steenwijkerkamp is a historically and scenically valuable area and must be preserved. Housing should be built at appropriate locations, without affecting cultural heritage and the character of Steenwijk. Preserve what is valuable, build with vision.

124100.explanation: Steenwijkerland already faces heavy tourist pressure. New holiday parks affect landscape and livability and offer little to local residents. The municipality should choose quality over quantity and first invest in existing parks and facilities.

124101.explanation: Wind turbines impact the landscape, livability, and residential pleasure and provide little local benefit. The municipality should not push through large-scale energy projects against residents’ wishes. Protect the landscape and choose solutions that fit the region.

124047.explanation: Major decisions require support among residents. Our 50/50 approach involves residents via a citizen forum and expert panel.

124051.explanation: Support for initiatives with local support. No obligation or forcing through without realistic feasibility.

124052.explanation: Only allow when there is real employment. No empty promises. Distribute well across the Netherlands.

124056.explanation: No extra bans on wood burning. Responsible use is important. Households cannot rely on electricity alone.

124058.explanation: Can be unifying for residents but must be paid for by residents or neighborhood themselves.

124059.explanation: The entire ROVA system must be improved; it is unfair for households. A low threshold is important but financially responsible.

124062.explanation: Sports promote health and participation; therefore, targeted support for low incomes is justified, with appropriate policies and through the Steenwijkerland Sports and Culture Fund.

124064.explanation: Good schools are the foundation, with school mergers to preserve education in every core, village, and town. Invest where needed and distribute resources fairly.

124065.explanation: Music education is valuable but basic facilities take priority. Support where financially feasible.

124068.explanation: Visible enforcement strengthens safety and livability.

124069.explanation: Only deploy where demonstrable nuisance exists. No standard expansion without necessity.

124074.explanation: Build where logical and fitting. Tailored approach per rural area.

124075.explanation: Social compensation strengthens involvement. Always tailored per situation.

124076.explanation: Protection of low incomes remains necessary. Vulnerable residents deserve support.

124077.explanation: There is already a good policy in Steenwijkerland.

124079.explanation: Facilitate where needed with freedom of choice for residents. No full push for electric only. Where residents come up with solutions themselves, do not impose restrictions.

124080.explanation: Traffic safety comes first. No generic measures without analysis.

124081.explanation: Freedom of choice and human scale in care. Respect for religion and culture.

124085.explanation: Protect competitive position and certain holiday parks and hotels already do not generate tourist tax due to accommodating refugees. Tourism is an economic pillar.

124089.explanation: Accessibility is important. Keep costs manageable but not free. The polluter pays.

124091.explanation: Increase safety and health. Deserves support for people who cannot afford it.

124094.explanation: Affordable housing is important but no fixed percentage. Tailored solutions per location and this is already in effect in Steenwijkerland. More attention for local residents and people with economic ties to the center.

124095.explanation: High-rise buildings only where appropriate, such as in Steenwijk and in other places where possible and fitting with the environment and landscape.

124096.explanation: Only regulate in case of nuisance. As long as there are too few social rental homes, this must be tolerated. Protect livability.

124098.explanation: Access is necessary for livability. Nature should not block necessary infrastructure. Ensure good communication with residents and stakeholders.

124099.explanation: No building on De Kamp. Heritage is not a leftover location as long as alternatives are available.

124100.explanation: We are open to new recreation parks if they contribute to the local economy and ensure livability throughout the year. Where possible within the infrastructure, without nuisance.

124101.explanation: No large wind turbines in Steenwijkerland. The Regional Energy Strategy is no longer up to date. Landscape protection comes first.

statements.title: No new holiday parks should be added in Steenwijkerland.
Residents of Steenwijkerland should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Steenwijkerland moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted in Steenwijkerland.

titleParts.text: In Steenwijkerland moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety should be employed in Steenwijkerland.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Housing should be built on the Steenwijkerkamp near Steenwijk.
More CCTV surveillance should be installed in Steenwijkerland.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality should invest millions in the bypass roads of Blokzijl and Ossenzijl.
Of the new homes built in Steenwijkerland, at least 30% should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Steenwijkerland moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: More high-rise residential apartment buildings should be allowed in Steenwijkerland.
Steenwijkerland should work towards the installation of several wind turbines.

titleParts.text: Steenwijkerland moet zich inzetten voor de plaatsing van enkele

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.
